theory.technical_interpretation: Technically, state space representation is interpreted through the variables and operators present in the canonical relation. The equation support section should preserve these source equations in sequence so later proof and Wolfram passes operate on the same mathematical object.

equation_progression.latex: To map an \(n $\times$ m\) real matrix (blob) to a DAANSphere address: (1) perform a singular value decomposition \(B = U\Sigma V^T\); extract the first three left singular vectors \(u_1, u_2, u_3\) and normalize them to points on \(S^2\); (2) convert the angles to continued fraction expansions, multiply each coefficient by \(\pi^{p/2}\) for the desired precision and extract digits in base 3; (3) apply the Cantor pairing function recursively to produce a single integer modulo 153{,}600; (4) select a tesseract vertex using the lower four bits; (5) finalize the address by combining the vertex and Cantor encoding; (6) prepare an 18-qubit state corresponding to the 18-bit binary representation of the address.\\

T_{\text{kinetic}} &= \tfrac{1}{2} g^{ij}[\Psi]\,\pi_i\pi_j,\ U_{\text{magnetic}} &= -\mu_{\mathrm{eff}}[\Psi]\cdot B_{\mathrm{ext}}(r_{\mathrm{cm}}(\Psi)) + \frac{\mu_0}{8\pi}\int_V (|B_{\text{dipole}}|^2 + |B_{\text{gyro}}|^2)\,\mathrm{d}^3 r,\ U_{\text{quantum}} &= \sum_n \hbar \omega_n\left(n_n + \tfrac{1}{2}\right) + U_{\text{tunnel}}[d(\Psi)],\ H_{\text{coupling}} &= \frac{g_{\text{factor}}\,\mu_B}{\hbar}\,L_{\text{gyro}}[\Psi]\cdot\mu_{\mathrm{eff}}[\Psi] + \lambda_{\text{coupling}}\,A[r_{\text{obs}}]\cdot J_{\text{gyro}}[\Psi].

reviewed_source_symbols.description: vacuum permeability normalization in the electromagnetic tensor expression.
density matrix or state-density variable in the entry relation.
potential function whose derivatives mark stationary and inflection conditions.
costate or momentum-like variable in the control relation.
gradient operator controlling spatial variation in the entry equation.
angular frequency or resonance parameter in the local relation.

dictionary_sections.definition:
state space representation is a source-backed registry entry whose production form is anchored to the reviewed equation \exists (
abla 	imes (ho_{charge} \mathbf{v}) 
eq 0 \land 
abla \cdot \mathbf{v} 
eq 0) \implies  rac{\partial \mathbf{B}}{\partial t} = 
abla 	imes (\mathbf{v} 	imes \mathbf{B}). The entry records the mathematical object, condition, or operator named by the title and prepares it for the locked TZPID four-page layout.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: In context, state space representation functions as a local mathematical checkpoint. The canonical equation gives the entry its printable formal spine, while the supporting equations show how the relation is used or constrained inside the surrounding registry arc.

dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: The interpretive role of state space representation is to make this mathematical condition available as a stable registry node, with dictionary explanation, symbol key, Wolfram form, derivation support, and proof certificate aligned to the same source evidence.

The entry belongs in the production corpus only with the reviewed formula and supporting relation kept together.
